2604.10441.title: VeriSim: A Configurable Framework for Evaluating Medical AI Under Realistic Patient Noise
2604.10441.paper_summary: Medical large language models (LLMs) achieve impressive performance on standardized benchmarks, yet these evaluations fail to capture the complexity of real clinical encounters where patients exhibit memory gaps, limited health literacy, anxiety, and other communication barriers. We introduce VeriSim, a truth-preserving patient simulation framework that injects controllable, clinically evidence-grounded noise into patient responses while maintaining strict adherence to medical ground truth through a hybrid UMLS-LLM verification mechanism. Our framework operationalizes six noise dimensions derived from peer-reviewed medical communication literature, capturing authentic clinical phenomena such as patient recall limitations, health literacy barriers, and stigma-driven non-disclosure. Experiments across seven open-weight LLMs reveal that all models degrade significantly under realistic patient noise, with diagnostic accuracy dropping 15-25% and conversation length increasing 34-55%. Notably, smaller models (7B) show 40% greater degradation than larger models (70B+), while medical fine-tuning on standard corpora provides limited robustness benefits against patient communication noise. Evaluation by board-certified clinicians demonstrates high-quality simulation with strong inter-annotator agreement (kappa > 0.80), while LLM-as-a-Judge serves as a validated auxiliary evaluator achieving comparable reliability for scalable assessment. Our results highlight a critical Sim-to-Real gap in current medical AI. We release VeriSim as an open-source noise-injection framework, establishing a rigorous testbed for evaluating clinical robustness.
2604.10441.paper_summary_zh: 醫療大型語言模型（LLMs）在標準化基準測試中表現出色，但這些評估未能捕捉到真實臨床接觸的複雜性，患者在這些情境中會出現記憶缺口、有限的健康素養、焦慮以及其他溝通障礙。我們介紹了VeriSim，一個保持真實性的患者模擬框架，該框架在患者反應中注入可控的、基於臨床證據的噪音，同時通過混合UMLS-LLM驗證機制嚴格遵循醫療真實性。我們的框架將來自同行評審的醫療溝通文獻的六個噪音維度進行了操作化，捕捉到真實的臨床現象，如患者回憶限制、健康素養障礙以及因污名驅動的非披露。對七個開放權重LLM的實驗顯示，所有模型在現實患者噪音下均顯著降級，診斷準確率下降15-25%，對話長度增加34-55%。值得注意的是，較小的模型（7B）顯示出比較大的模型（70B+）高出40%的降級，而在標準語料上進行的醫療微調對抗患者溝通噪音的穩健性益處有限。由董事會認證的臨床醫生進行的評估顯示出高質量的模擬，並且標註者之間的一致性強（kappa > 0.80），同時LLM作為評判者作為經過驗證的輔助評估者，實現了可擴展評估的可比可靠性。我們的結果突顯了當前醫療AI中的一個關鍵Sim-to-Real差距。我們將VeriSim作為開源噪音注入框架發布，建立了一個嚴謹的測試平台，用於評估臨床穩健性。
2604.10441.author: Sina Mansouri et.al.
2604.10441.authors: Sina Mansouri, Mohit Marvania, Vibhavari Ashok Shihorkar, Han Ngoc Tran, Kazhal Shafiei, Mehrdad Fazli, Yikuan Li, Ziwei Zhu
